home.welcomeText: Discover the magnificent summer residence of the last Emir of Bukhara

gallery.title: Photo Gallery
gallery.subtitle: Explore the beauty of Sitorai Mohi Xosa

hero.subtitle: A palace befitting the stars and the moon

story.headLead: A name written to look like
story.p1: Sitorai Mohi Xossa — «a palace befitting the stars and the moon». Built by the Manghit dynasty as the country residence of the last emirs of Bukhara, four kilometres north of the city on the road from Samarkand, with its first stones laid under Amir Nasrulla-khan and its final shape given under Amir Abdulahad Bahodir-khan.
story.p2: Three rulers, three master architects — Hoji Hafiz, Ostonqul Hafizov, Mirzo Ustomiddin Sarkor — and a generation of carvers, painters and silversmiths from Bukhara. From 1912 to 1918 the European volumes met the Eastern decorative grammar in these walls. A century on, peacocks still cross the rose gardens.
story.caption: “The East and the West meet at every step.”

hall.body: Carved white ganch laid over mirror — a technique attempted here for the first time. No pattern is ever repeated. The mirrors return a reflection forty times over, until the room dissolves into something close to a dream.
hall.credit: By the hand of Usto Shirin Muradov & thirty masters.

collection.title: What the walls still keep
collection.helperWeb: Over a hundred pieces survive in the halls. Hover a piece, then open it.
collection.helperMobile: Over a hundred pieces survive in the halls. Tap a piece, then open it.

visit.title: Cross the gardens at golden hour
visit.colophon: Sitorai Mohi Xosa — Museum of Decorative & Applied Arts · est. 1927

quiz.question: Question {n} / {total}
quiz.verdictAll: A keeper of the palace’s secrets.
quiz.verdictHalf: A patient eye — and a few more visits to make.
quiz.verdictSome: A glance through the doorway. Come back to look longer.
quiz.verdictNone: The palace asks for a slower visit. Try again.

museums.title: Museums of the complex
museums.intro: Walk three connected pavilions — the Reception Palace, the eight-room Costume Hall and the Harem — each its own museum within Sitorai Mohi Xosa.

salomxona.name: Salomxona
salomxona.subtitle: Reception Palace · 1912–1914
salomxona.desc: Main reception pavilion built under Emir Sayyid Olim Khan. Houses the Chinese-Japanese porcelain collection, silver, late-19th-c European furniture, and the carved kursi-throne.

xonai-xasht.name: Xonai Xasht
xonai-xasht.subtitle: Eight-room Costume Hall
xonai-xasht.desc: Eight-room pavilion turned into the Museum of 19th- and 20th-century Bukharan national costume — chapans of every social class, brides’ dresses, ikat, gold-thread zarduzi.

harem.name: Harem
harem.subtitle: Private quarters · ceramics & suzani
harem.desc: The harem hall — Bukharan Gijduvan ceramics (Ibadulla Narzullayev), large suzani embroideries, women’s costumes and the painted-ceiling rooms.
